---
title: "Midterm Exercise"


author:
  - name: "Carsten Lange"
    email: "clange@cpp.edu"
    affiliation: "Professor of Economics, Cal Poly, Pomona"


format: live-html

webr:
  packages: ['dplyr', 'ggplot2', 'janitor', 'TeachHist'] #Auto-install packages

engine: knitr
---

{{< include ../../_extensions/r-wasm/live/_knitr.qmd >}}

{{< include ../../_extensions/r-wasm/live/_gradethis.qmd >}}

::: {.content-visible when-runtime="loading"}
::: {.callout-note}
## Please be Patient: Loading R Environment
The interactive R environment and the required packages must be loaded in your browser. This typically takes 1–2 minutes. You can monitor the progress in the bottom-right corner. 

When everything is loaded, you get a **"Ready to roll"** message below.
Thank you for your patience!
:::
:::

```{webr}
#| auto-run: true
#| echo: false
cat("\033[1;32mReady to roll\033[0m\n")
```



**Libraries** already loaded in the background:

- `tidyverse`
- `janitor`
- `ggplot2`
- `TeachHist`



The code below loaded the Titanic dataset already into the data frame `DataTitanic`. Below the code you find the first six observations.
```{webr}
#| label: exc_all
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
DataTitanic = read.csv("https://ai.lange-analytics.com/data/Titanic.csv") |>
                 clean_names("upper_camel") |>
                 mutate(Survived=as.factor(Survived) )

DataTitanicYoungstersCorrect=DataTitanic |>
                  select(Survived, PasClass=Pclass, Age, FareInPounds) |> 
                  filter(Age<25) |> 
                  mutate(FareInPoundsPlusTax=1.05*FareInPounds)

head(DataTitanic)
```

## Exercise 1

Completet the pipe below for the following tasks and save it into the new data frame 'DataTitanicYoungsters':

1. Select the variables:
    - Survived
    - Pclass (rename it to PasClass)
    - Age
    - FareInPounds

2. Filter the data to only include passengers younger than 25 years

3. Create a new variable `FareInPoundsPlusTax` by multiplying `FareInPounds` by 1.05

4. Calculate the mean age for passengers younger than 25 years old


```{webr}
#| exercise: exc_1
#| exercise.setup: exc_all
DataTitanicYoungsters=DataTitanic |>
                  ______(______, ______, ______, ______) |> 
                  ______(______) |> 
                  ______(______)
head(DataTitanicYoungsters)

MeanFareInPoundsInPlusTax=mean(______)
cat("Avg. Fare in Pounds including tax:", MeanFareInPoundsInPlusTax)
```


## Exercise 2

Use the data frame `DataTitanicYoungsterCorrect` and create a ggplot point graph with `Age` at the horizontal axis, `FareInCurrenDollars` at the vertical axis and color code the points according to if a passenger `Survived`. 

Also add a linear trend line without a standard error band.

Finally, add the following labels: 

**Title:** `"Diagram Age and Fare"`<br>
**x Axis Label:** `"Age in Years"`<br>
**y Axis Label:** `"Fare in Current U.S. Dollars"` 

Complete the code below for these tasks:

```{webr}
#| exercise: exc_2
#| exercise.setup: exc_all
PlotTitanic=ggplot(aes(x=______, y=______),  
                   data=______)+
            geom_point(aes(______=______))+
            geom_smooth(se=FALSE, method="______")+
            labs(______,
                 ______,
                 ______)

plot(PlotTitanic)
```



## Exercise 3

The mean height of men is $70$ inches with a standard deviation of $3$ inches.

What is the probabitity of a random man to have a height between 69 inches and 71 inches. Complete  the R ode below to get your answer.

```{webr}
#| auto-run: true
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
TeachHistDens(70,3, VLine1=69, VLine2=71)
```

```{webr}
#| exercise: exc_3
P2=pnorm(______, ______, ______)
P1=pnorm(______, ______, ______)
Answer=______
cat("The probability for a man beeing between 69 and 71 inches is:", Answer)
```


## Exercise 4

The mean height of men is $70$ inches with a standard deviation of $3$ inches.

What is the height excceeded by only 3% of all men.

```{webr}
#| auto-run: true
#| echo: false
#| warning: false
#| message: false
TeachHistDens(70,3, VLine1=75.64)
```

```{webr}
#| exercise: exc_4
QHeight=qnorm(______, ______, ______)
cat("The height exceeded by only 1% of all men is:", QHeight, "Inches")
```
